circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: The Yas Marina Circuit is a 5. 281 km track located on Yas Island in Abu Dhabi, designed by Hermann Tilke, which has been the venue for Formula One's season-closing Abu Dhabi Grand Prix since 2009. It represents the second Middle Eastern Formula One circuit to be built, following Bahrain's establishment and preceding later additions in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: The Albert Park Circuit is a street track encircling Albert Park Lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb, hosting the Australian Grand Prix as part of the Formula One calendar along with various supporting categories. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and annually features the Supercars Championship Melbourne SuperSprint and additional support events.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit has hosted the Austrian Grand Prix across two distinct periods: initially as the Österreichring from 1970 to 1987, and later in its redesigned form from 1997 to 2003. The venue underwent significant modifications between these eras, including a shortening and reconstruction of the track layout.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: Baku City Circuit is a street track in Azerbaijan's capital positioned along the city boulevard, stretching 6. 003 km per lap and ranking as the fourth-longest course in Formula One. The venue first hosted a world championship race in 2016 with the European Grand Prix, then adopted the Azerbaijan Grand Prix name from 2017 onwards.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, the Bahrain International Circuit spans 5. 412 km and has hosted the annual Formula One Bahrain Grand Prix, marking the championship's first Middle Eastern venue. The FIA Grade 1-licensed facility accommodates various motorsport disciplines including drag racing, sports car endurance competitions, and formerly the Australian V8 Supercars series, while featuring multiple circuit configurations.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya spans 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced mix of extended straights and diverse corner configurations, making it a versatile test for all racing disciplines. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: Spa-Francorchamps is a 7. 004 km Belgian circuit in Wallonia that has served as the venue for the Formula One Belgian Grand Prix since 1950, including the championship's inaugural season. The track has hosted the race annually from 1985 onward, with only two exceptions in 2003 and 2006.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, the 4. 361-kilometre Circuit Gilles Villeneuve serves as the home of Canada's Formula One Grand Prix. Beyond its role as an F1 venue, the track has welcomed various international motorsport series including sports car championships and North American racing competitions.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: The Shanghai International Circuit in Jiading District hosted China's premier Formula One event from 2004 to 2019, then again beginning in 2024. The venue has expanded its motorsport calendar to include electric racing competitions alongside the Chinese Grand Prix.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Located in England near Northamptonshire, Silverstone Circuit has been the principal venue for Britain's Formula One Grand Prix since 1948 and notably hosted the inaugural World Championship race in 1950. After sharing the British Grand Prix with two rival circuits between 1955 and 1986, Silverstone became its permanent home from 1987 onward.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring near Budapest became the first Formula One circuit behind the Iron Curtain when it hosted its inaugural Grand Prix in 1986, making Hungarian racing history. Built in just eight months starting from late 1985, this 4. 381 km track was constructed outside the capital near a major highway after plans for a street circuit in Budapest's central park were rejected by the government.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: Located near Milan in northern Italy, the Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km track built in 1922 as mainland Europe's oldest purpose-built racing circuit. The venue, nicknamed "the Temple of Speed" for its extended straights and fast corners, has continuously hosted the Italian Grand Prix since 1949, save for a closure in 1980 for renovation work.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: The Suzuka Circuit is a 5. 807 km Japanese motorsport facility operated by Honda Mobilityland in Mie Prefecture, hosting a capacity of 155,000 spectators. It serves as the primary venue for Formula One racing in Japan and is equally prominent in the Super Formula championship series.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street track located in Paradise, Nevada, that navigates through the famous Las Vegas Boulevard and its surrounding urban landscape. The 3. 8-mile course passes by iconic venues such as the MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas, making it one of the most visually distinctive racing layouts on the Formula One calendar.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: Located in Mexico City, the Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez is a 4. 304 km circuit named after two tragic racing brothers, Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, who died in separate incidents during the 1960s and 1970s. The track has hosted the Formula One Mexican Grand Prix since 2015 on its current configuration, though it previously staged the race on different layouts during two earlier periods ending in 1992.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary racing venue constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, featuring a 3. 363-mile circuit with 19 turns and qualifying speeds averaging 139 mph. Designed specifically for Formula One by specialist circuit architects, the track was introduced to the championship calendar in 2022.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco is a compact 3. 337 km street course that winds through the Monte Carlo and La Condamine districts of Monaco, following the natural contours around the harbour. Though officially named after the principality itself, it is widely known by the Monte Carlo designation due to the circuit's predominant location within that neighbourhood.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch coastal dunes near Amsterdam, the 4. 259-kilometre Zandvoort circuit hosted the Formula One Dutch Grand Prix from 2021 onwards following its return to the calendar after a long absence. The venue's partnership with the sport is scheduled to conclude in 2026.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: The Lusail International Circuit is positioned north of Doha in Qatar and holds an FIA Grade 1 license. The 5. 419 km layout features a 1.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Interlagos is a 4. 309 km Brazilian motor racing circuit in São Paulo that opened in 1940. The track was renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula One driver José Carlos Pace, who perished in an aviation accident eight years earlier, and has served as his burial site since 2024.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street track positioned along Saudi Arabia's Red Sea waterfront that has served as the venue for the Formula One Saudi Arabian Grand Prix since its introduction in 2021, and began hosting Formula E competition in 2025.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit winds through Singapore's downtown area and Kallang district, utilizing the streets surrounding Marina Bay as its racing layout.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located near Barcelona in northeastern Spain, this 4. 657-kilometer facility is regarded as a versatile racing venue with its combination of extended straights and diverse corner configurations. The track accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands and holds the highest FIA safety certification level.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: The Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas is a 3. 426-mile FIA Grade 1 facility that serves as the permanent home of the Formula One United States Grand Prix. Beyond F1, the venue hosts major motorsport events including NASCAR, MotoGP's Motorcycle Grand Prix of the Americas, and the Lone Star Le Mans endurance race, while previously accommodating numerous other racing series.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a Thai-British driver currently competing for Williams in Formula One, competing under Thailand's flag.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Fernando Alonso, a Spanish driver currently racing for Aston Martin, is a two-time Formula One World Champion whose victories came in 2005 and 2006 during his tenure at Renault. Beyond his 32 career Grand Prix wins across more than two decades in the sport, Alonso has achieved notable success in endurance racing, including World Endurance Championship glory and multiple victories at prestigious events like Le Mans and Daytona, making him the sole driver to have captured both a Formula One World Championship and a World Endurance Championship.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli has established himself in Formula One with Mercedes, achieving five Grand Prix victories following his entry into the sport in 2025.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Haas, competing at the highest level of motorsport.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Gabi Bortoleto, a Brazilian motorsport competitor, currently races in Formula One as a member of the Audi team.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Finnish driver Valtteri Bottas has secured ten Grand Prix victories throughout his 13-year Formula One career, including two consecutive runner-up finishes in the drivers' championship with Mercedes in 2019 and 2020. He currently competes for Cadillac in the series.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Franco Colapinto is an Argentine racing driver currently competing in Formula One with the Alpine team.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Australian driver Jack Doohan currently holds a reserve position at Haas while competing in European Le Mans Series endurance racing. He has participated in seven Formula One races between 2024 and 2025.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: French driver Pierre Gasly currently races for Alpine in Formula One, having previously achieved a notable victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix during his tenure with AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar, a driver of French and Algerian descent, races for Red Bull Racing in Formula One under the French flag.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Ferrari who has matched Michael Schumacher's record of seven world championship titles. He holds multiple track records including the most race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes in the sport's history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: Nicolas Hülkenberg is a German driver currently racing in Formula One for Audi. Beyond his single-seater commitments, he achieved success in sports car racing, securing victory at the 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 while driving for Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson is a New Zealand-born racing driver currently competing in Formula One for Racing Bulls.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc is a Monégasque driver competing for Ferrari in Formula One, having finished second in the 2022 championship standings. Across his nine seasons in the sport, he has secured nine Grand Prix victories.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Danish driver Kevin Magnussen, known as K-Mag, has transitioned away from full-time Formula One competition, which he pursued across two separate stints between 2014 and 2024. He now focuses on endurance racing with BMW's factory team in the World Endurance Championship, while also competing part-time in NASCAR's top series.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver competing for McLaren, secured the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship with the team. Across his eight seasons in the sport, he has accumulated 11 Grand Prix victories.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon, a French motorsport competitor, currently drives for Haas in Formula One. His career highlights include a victory at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix when he was racing for Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Mexican driver Sergio Pérez competes for Cadillac in Formula One after spending the previous season with Red Bull, where he finished second in the 2023 championship standings. Over his 15-year Formula One career, Pérez has accumulated six Grand Prix victories.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri is an Australian Formula One driver currently racing for McLaren, having accumulated nine Grand Prix victories over the course of four seasons in the sport.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Australian driver Daniel Ricciardo competed in Formula One between 2011 and 2024, earning the nickname "the Honey Badger" throughout his 14-season career. He achieved eight Grand Prix victories during his time in the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: British driver George Russell currently races for Mercedes in Formula One, where he has secured seven race victories over his eight seasons in the sport.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz competes for Williams in Formula One, having secured four Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American driver who raced in Formula One during 2023 and 2024 before transitioning to sports car racing. He currently competes in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category for Proton Competition, having previously driven LMP2 machinery in IMSA competition.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian Formula One driver currently competing for Aston Martin in the championship.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda is a Japanese driver who has held reserve positions with Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls throughout his Formula One involvement from 2021 to 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: Max Verstappen is a Dutch-Belgian driver competing for Red Bull Racing who has claimed four consecutive World Drivers' Championship titles spanning from 2021 through 2024. Over his 12-season Formula One career, he has accumulated 71 Grand Prix victories.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu holds the distinction of being the sole Chinese competitor in Formula One history, having raced in the sport between 2022 and 2024. Currently, he functions as a reserve driver for the Cadillac Formula One team.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: The Enstone-based team has competed as Alpine since 2021, when Renault rebranded its works outfit to showcase its sports car division before withdrawing from the sport at the end of 2025. Powered by Mercedes engines and operating under a French licence, Alpine secured its only victory through Esteban Ocon's triumph at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin, a British automotive manufacturer, has maintained a presence in Formula One across multiple eras under different organizational structures, with its contemporary iteration competing under the AMR GP Limited banner.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker Ferrari, stands as Formula One's most accomplished and longest-tenured outfit, fielding cars in every championship season from 1950 onward. The squad, informally called "the Prancing Horse" after its iconic emblem, remains unmatched in its longevity and record of success within the sport.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: Gene Haas founded this American Formula One team in 2014, launching its racing programme in 2016 with a focus on competitive entry-level performance. Following Guenther Steiner's departure after nearly a decade leading the outfit, Ayao Komatsu assumed the team principal position in early 2024.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Founded by Peter Sauber in 1970, this Swiss engineering firm established itself through endurance racing success before joining Formula One in 1993 as an independent constructor. The team competed in the sport for over three decades, becoming one of the longest-serving constructors in the championship's history, until transitioning to Audi ownership in 2024 ahead of its final season in 2025.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing is a British Formula One outfit headquartered at its Woking facility, operating as a subsidiary within the McLaren Group structure under Bahraini ownership through Mumtalakat Holding Company.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes-Benz has maintained a presence in Formula One since 1954 in both ownership and engine supply roles. The current iteration, Mercedes-AMG Petronas Formula One Team, operates from Brackley under a German licence with shared ownership between Mercedes-Benz Group, Ineos, and team principal Toto Wolff as of 2022. The marque is traditionally associated with the "Silver Arrows" nickname in racing.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that commenced competition in 2024. Based in Faenza with a secondary facility near Milton Keynes, it operates as one of two constructors under Red Bull GmbH's ownership, serving alongside Red Bull Racing as part of the parent company's multi-team Formula One program.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that entered the sport in 2024 as one of two Red Bull-owned outfits. Based in Faenza with a Milton Keynes facility, it operates as a sister team to Red Bull Racing under Austrian parent company Red Bull GmbH.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Red Bull Racing is an Austrian-licensed Formula One team headquartered in England and owned by the Red Bull beverage company alongside the Racing Bulls squad. Established in 2005, the team was under Christian Horner's management for two decades before he departed in 2025, succeeded by Laurent Mekies as team principal.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering Limited, now competing as Atlassian Williams F1 Team, represents a British Formula One constructor established in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, following the failure of Williams's previous racing venture. Headquartered at a sprawling facility in Grove, Oxfordshire, the team has maintained its operations from this 60-acre site since its inception.
